objective: Plan the selected-profile aggregate M12 verifier over the AIIP channel, terms and semantic negotiation, federated admission, recovery, and portable exit slices.
falsifiable_claim: The selected M12 profile preserves each System's local completeness, prevents peer truth or authority mutation and direct foreign physical control, and exports permitted participant state without host trust, or fails without advancing the M13 prerequisite.
exit_criteria: Each System remains locally complete when it declines or disconnects, and neither peer can mutate the other's truth or widen its authority.

The aggregate conformance matrix rejects direct foreign physical-control streams plus identity, profile, terms, root, sequence, ordering, conflict, recovery, authority, and portable-state substitutions.

External participants retain permitted portable state without continued access to an IOI-hosted room, and protocol completion does not imply verification, acceptance, settlement, correctness, or positive economic value.
Retained evidence log contains exactly one literal M12_EXIT=0; process exit status alone is insufficient.
adversarial_or_fault_proof: Run the aggregate channel, terms, semantic, federated-admission, recovery, disconnect, decline, peer-mutation, authority-widening, foreign-control, protocol-claim, and portable-exit matrices with exact predecessor and root substitutions.
remaining_nonclaims: AIIP completion establishes no verification, acceptance, settlement, correctness, positive-surplus, marketplace, network-effect, or Internet-of-Intelligence claim.
This proposed verifier activates no M13 work and closes no stage.
source_provenance: Plan-level proposal derived from docs/architecture/_meta/work-items/m0-m14-plan-gap-audit.md at origin/master 69592149186cb29383a397ad0aa3ad6f5ab4ab7b; status transaction 2026-07-22.
